base.name: White Oak
base.description: The steadfast White Oak is the grovekeeper's oldest friend — deep-rooted, generous with timber, and patient as the hills. A single acorn planted in honest soil will outlive the one who planted it.
base.codexHint: Your first tree is already waiting. Plant one to begin your grove.

base.name: Weeping Willow
base.description: The Weeping Willow trails its silver-green curtains in any nearby stream, drinking deeply and repaying the kindness with sap so sweet it drips like honey on a warm afternoon.
base.codexHint: Plant near water tiles to unlock this species' true potential.

base.name: Elder Pine
base.description: Elder Pines stand sentinel on the mountain slopes year-round, their dark needles dusted with frost in winter yet never losing heart. Where other trees sleep, the Elder Pine simply breathes slower.
base.codexHint: One of the few trees that keeps growing when the frost arrives.

base.name: Cherry Blossom
base.description: When a Cherry Blossom opens its first flowers, the whole grove seems to hold its breath — and then a soft pink blizzard of petals drifts across everything, filling the air with quiet wonder.
base.codexHint: Reach level 5 and let spring's beauty inspire every tree around it.

base.name: Ghost Birch
base.description: The Ghost Birch's papery silver bark carries a faint inner light that brightens as the moon rises, making a grove of them look like a gathering of lanterns on winter nights.
base.codexHint: Found at the tundra's edge — the cold only makes its glow more radiant.

base.name: Redwood
base.description: There is something humbling about standing beside a Redwood — its trunk broad as a cottage wall, its crown lost in morning mist, holding in its heartwood the memory of a hundred seasons.
base.codexHint: A long investment, but an Old Growth Redwood also rewards you with acorns.

base.name: Flame Maple
base.description: Come autumn, the Flame Maple earns its name: every leaf ignites in shades of amber, crimson, and gold, and neighboring trees seem to grow more fruitful just from basking in its warmth.
base.codexHint: Autumn is its season — harvest then for double the fruit.

base.name: Baobab
base.description: Ancient and unhurried, the Baobab stores enough water in its swollen trunk to weather months of drought, sharing its surplus with smaller plants sheltering in its shadow.
base.codexHint: The most generous tree in the grove — timber, sap, and fruit, all at once.

base.name: Silver Birch
base.description: Slender and quick-footed, the Silver Birch is first to colonise a bare patch of earth, its white bark catching every slant of sunlight and its roots drinking happily beside the nearest stream.
base.codexHint: One of the fastest growers in the grove, especially near water.

base.name: Ironbark
base.description: Ironbark earned its name honestly — the deeply furrowed grey-black bark is as hard as the mountain it grows on, shrugging off storms that would topple anything else.
base.codexHint: Plant it where winds are fiercest; storms cannot touch an Ironbark.
base.special: Storm immune; 3x timber at Old Growth

base.name: Golden Apple
base.description: In the peak of harvest, the Golden Apple hangs boughs so heavy with shining fruit that the branches nearly touch the ground, filling the whole orchard with the warm scent of ripe sweetness.
base.codexHint: Autumn harvest yields triple fruit — patience is delicious.

base.name: Mystic Fern
base.description: The Mystic Fern does not merely grow beside other trees — it listens to them, weaving its fine roots through theirs and quietly sharing energy, so a crowded grove makes it flourish beyond all expectation.
base.codexHint: Surround it with neighbours — every adjacent tree makes it grow faster.

prestige.name: Crystalline Oak
prestige.description: The Crystalline Oak exists somewhere between tree and gemstone — its translucent leaves catch the light and scatter it in prismatic arcs that shift with every passing season, as if the tree itself cannot decide which colour to be.
prestige.codexHint: A gift of the prestige path, bestowed on grovekeepers who begin again.

prestige.name: Moonwood Ash
prestige.description: The Moonwood Ash sleeps through daylight hours, then stirs at dusk, its pale canopy unfurling under starlight and breathing out a silver shimmer that floats like mist between the other trees.
prestige.codexHint: It only grows in darkness — a night-watch tree for the most dedicated grovekeeper.
prestige.special: Grows only at night; silver shimmer

prestige.name: Worldtree
prestige.description: At the heart of every great forest, there was once a Worldtree — its roots threading the underground rivers, its branches wide enough to shelter a village, its presence drawing every living thing closer together.
prestige.codexHint: Plant it at the centre of a complete grove and feel the valley breathe again.
